==================================================================
  APERIO-LITE  —  HOW TO INSTALL & USE
  One brain. Every agent. Nothing forgotten.
==================================================================

Aperio runs entirely on your own computer.
No cloud. No subscriptions. No data leaving your machine
(unless you choose a cloud AI provider yourself during setup).

For the full, friendlier guide see how-to-install.md, or open the
in-app Help page after setup.

------------------------------------------------------------------
  BEFORE YOU START
------------------------------------------------------------------

  Operating system : macOS, Linux, or Windows
  Internet         : Needed only during first-time setup (to fetch
                     the AI engine, a model, and dependencies).
  Offline?         : Everything works offline after setup. Only
                     web search / fetch_url need the internet.
  Disk space       : 3-20 GB free, depending on the model chosen.
  RAM              : 4 GB minimum. More RAM = a smarter model is
                     picked automatically.

  No technical knowledge required. A launcher starts Aperio, then a
  setup page opens in your browser and does the rest. First-time
  setup takes 5-15 minutes, mostly model download time.

  Download the latest release:
    https://github.com/BaiGanio/aperio/releases/latest/download/aperio-lite.zip
  Questions & support:
    https://github.com/BaiGanio/aperio/discussions/14

  Unzip aperio-lite.zip anywhere. The folder that comes out IS the
  app - everything Aperio installs lives inside it.

------------------------------------------------------------------
  HOW TO RUN - FIRST TIME
------------------------------------------------------------------

  WINDOWS
    1. Open the Aperio folder.
    2. Double-click START.bat
    3. If Windows warns "Unknown publisher", click
       More info -> Run anyway (expected for unsigned apps).
    4. Your browser opens the setup page. Follow it.
    A shortcut "Aperio" is added to your Desktop for next time.

  macOS / LINUX
    1. Open a terminal in the Aperio folder.
    2. Run:   bash START.sh
    3. Your browser opens the setup page. Follow it.
    A launcher "Aperio" is added to your Desktop for next time.

  Keep the launcher window open while using Aperio - it is the
  engine. If the browser doesn't open, go to:
    http://localhost:31337

------------------------------------------------------------------
  WHAT THE SETUP DOES
------------------------------------------------------------------

  1. Choose local (free & private, recommended) or a cloud AI
     (paste one API key: Anthropic, DeepSeek, or Gemini).
  2. Local: Aperio installs its OWN copy of the llama.cpp engine
     inside its folder (vendor/llamacpp, not system-wide) and picks
     a model that fits your computer, downloading it once.
  3. A local SQLite database + a small on-device embedding model
     power Aperio's memory and search. No extra downloads.
  4. Done - Aperio opens and is ready.

------------------------------------------------------------------
  EVERY RUN AFTER THE FIRST
------------------------------------------------------------------

  Double-click the "Aperio" Desktop icon (Windows/macOS), or run
  bash START.sh again (Linux). Setup is skipped; ready in seconds.

------------------------------------------------------------------
  HOW TO STOP
------------------------------------------------------------------

  Click Quit in the app, or close the launcher window
  (or press Ctrl+C in it). The server stops automatically.

------------------------------------------------------------------
  UNINSTALL
------------------------------------------------------------------

  Everything is contained in the Aperio folder, except Node.js
  (kept in case you use it elsewhere).

  Windows      : double-click uninstall.bat
  macOS/Linux  : run  bash uninstall.sh

  The uninstaller stops the server, removes Aperio's engine,
  dependencies, database and logs, deletes the Desktop launcher,
  and offers to delete the downloaded model. Then delete the
  Aperio folder itself.

------------------------------------------------------------------
  TROUBLESHOOTING
------------------------------------------------------------------

  Setup page looks broken / buttons do nothing
    -> You opened setup.html directly (file://). Close it and start
       Aperio with START.bat / bash START.sh instead. Correct
       address: http://localhost:31337

  Port 31337 already in use
    -> Aperio may already be running - open http://localhost:31337.
       Otherwise close the old launcher window and start again.

  AI responses are very slow
    -> The model may be large for your RAM. Switch to a smaller
       local model in Settings -> Configuration.

==================================================================
  Aperio-lite - built for people, not pipelines.
==================================================================
